articles.title: $50M EVO Entertainment Destination Breaks Ground in Downtown Clearwater
articles.summary: A $50 million EVO Entertainment complex is moving forward in downtown Clearwater, Florida, with the site now cleared for development. EVO Entertainment is one of the fastest-growing upscale FEC brands in the country, combining bowling, arcades, movies, and dining under one roof. This project signals continued investor confidence in large-format entertainment destinations in urban cores.

articles.title: Intercard Cashless Technology Chosen for Legends Entertainment Arcade at Scheels Sports Park
articles.summary: Intercard has been selected to power the cashless arcade experience at the new 46,000-square-foot Legends Entertainment facility in Springfield, Illinois, which serves sports tournament visitors at the Legacy Pointe mixed-use development. The deal highlights the growing trend of FECs anchoring sports tourism complexes to capture family spending during tournament weekends. Cashless card systems remain a key revenue and operational efficiency driver for FEC operators.

articles.title: West Des Moines Approves Plans for Major Waterpark and Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: West Des Moines has approved a new large-scale waterpark and entertainment complex development, with updated plans now public. The project reflects ongoing municipal appetite for mixed entertainment destinations that drive tourism and tax revenue. FEC operators and developers should monitor this as a case study in navigating local approval processes for major entertainment investments.

articles.title: West Des Moines City Council Approves Development Agreement for Grand Experience Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: The West Des Moines city council has formally approved the development agreement for the Grand Experience project, a large-scale entertainment and waterpark destination. Municipal backing signals a strong public-private partnership model increasingly used to bring major FEC projects to market. This approval clears the way for construction to begin on what could become a regional entertainment anchor.

articles.title: The Breakers Resort Plans Upscale Family Entertainment Center in Palm Beach
articles.summary: The iconic Breakers resort in Palm Beach is planning a new Family Entertainment Center, with renderings now released publicly. The project illustrates the growing trend of luxury hospitality properties adding FEC amenities to boost on-property guest engagement and dwell time. FEC designers and suppliers should take note of how premium resort clients are approaching this space.

articles.title: Inside The Breakers' Family Entertainment Center Plans for Palm Beach
articles.summary: Additional coverage of The Breakers' planned FEC offers more detail on the facility's design and positioning within the luxury resort market. The project represents an emerging segment where high-end hospitality brands invest in structured family entertainment as a core amenity rather than an afterthought. Operators and designers targeting the resort FEC niche will find this a valuable benchmark.

articles.title: Sally Dark Rides Debuts Kong x Godzilla: The Ride at Lotte World Adventure
articles.summary: Sally Dark Rides, in collaboration with Legendary Entertainment and Toho, has opened KONG X GODZILLA: THE RIDE at Lotte World Adventure in South Korea on July 24. The multimedia dark ride is the first attraction to bring Legendary's Monsterverse IP into a fully immersive theme park experience. This launch demonstrates the continued appetite for IP-driven immersive attractions and Sally's expanding global footprint.

articles.title: Monsterverse Kong and Godzilla Dark Ride Opens at Lotte World Adventure
articles.summary: The KONG X GODZILLA: THE RIDE attraction has opened at Lotte World Adventure, marking Legendary Entertainment's first-ever theme park ride experience. Developed with Sally Dark Rides, the project showcases how entertainment studios are leveraging theme park partnerships to extend franchise value. For FEC operators exploring IP licensing, this is a high-profile example of how major brands are entering the attractions space.

articles.title: Developer Behind Proposed $3B Romulus Sports Complex Charged with Fraud
articles.summary: The developer behind a massive $3 billion sports and entertainment complex proposed for Romulus, Michigan has been charged with fraud, throwing the high-profile project into serious doubt. The case is a cautionary tale for municipalities and investors evaluating large-scale entertainment destination proposals. FEC industry stakeholders should scrutinize developer track records carefully before committing to anchor tenant or partnership agreements.

articles.title: Tornado Terry's Family Entertainment Center Closes Permanently
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's, a well-known family entertainment venue, is shutting down for good according to local Dallas-Fort Worth news. The closure is a reminder of the ongoing operational pressures facing independent FEC operators in a competitive entertainment market. Industry observers should watch for asset sales or lease opportunities that often follow such closures.

articles.title: Fort Worth Family Entertainment Favorite Closes After 35 Years
articles.summary: A beloved Fort Worth family entertainment center is permanently closing its doors after 35 years of operation, citing unspecified challenges after decades serving the local community. Long-tenured independent FECs face growing pressure from larger format competitors and evolving guest expectations. The closure raises important questions about succession planning and reinvestment cycles for legacy operators.

articles.title: Sky Zone Trampoline Park Expansion Brings New Jobs and Family Entertainment to Local Market
articles.summary: A new Sky Zone Trampoline Park opening is bringing jobs and family entertainment options to its community, continuing the brand's aggressive national expansion. Sky Zone remains one of the most active trampoline park franchisors, and new location openings serve as useful benchmarks for operators evaluating market saturation and site selection. This story also highlights the economic development angle FEC brands can leverage with local governments.

articles.title: Altitude Trampoline Park Opens New Location in Litchfield Park, Arizona
articles.summary: Altitude Trampoline Park is opening a new facility in Litchfield Park, Arizona, adding to the brand's growing national footprint. The opening reflects continued franchise expansion in the trampoline park segment despite market maturation in some regions. Operators and investors tracking the trampoline park category will want to note Altitude's continued growth activity in the Southwest.

articles.title: Lefty's Alley and Eats Opens New Bowling and Dining Concept in Newark, Delaware
articles.summary: Lefty's Alley and Eats is opening a new bowling alley and dining venue in Newark, Delaware, representing the continued growth of the eatertainment bowling segment. The concept combines food and beverage with bowling in a format that has proven resilient and popular with a broad demographic. New openings in this category provide useful data points for operators benchmarking their own F&B and entertainment mix.

articles.title: New Family Amusement Center with Restaurant and Bar Opens in Charleston Area
articles.summary: A new family amusement center combining attractions with a full restaurant and bar concept is opening in the Charleston, South Carolina area. The eatertainment model continues to gain traction as FEC operators seek to drive longer dwell times and higher per-capita spending through integrated F&B offerings. This opening is a useful example of how new entrants are designing venues for the current market.

articles.title: Record-Breaking Tag 4 A Cause Fundraiser Raises Over $2,500 for Alzheimer's Association
articles.summary: The largest Tag 4 A Cause fundraiser ever held raised more than $2,500 for the Alzheimer's Association, Central New York Chapter, held during the Laserforce Internationals tournament at The Fun Warehouse. The event demonstrates how laser tag venues can generate goodwill, community engagement, and publicity through cause-related programming. FEC operators looking to deepen local ties should consider structured charity event frameworks as a repeatable programming model.

articles.title: Fusion Family Fun Center Grand Opening Set for Bremen
articles.summary: Fusion Family Fun Center is holding a grand opening ribbon cutting in Bremen, adding a new FEC to the local market. New FEC openings in smaller markets reflect the ongoing expansion of the industry beyond major metro areas. Operators and suppliers targeting secondary and tertiary markets will find this opening relevant to understanding demand in those communities.

articles.title: Elevation Station FEC Returns to Covington, Louisiana with New Location
articles.summary: Elevation Station, a family entertainment center brand, is reopening in Covington, Louisiana at a new location after a previous presence in the market. Rebranding and relocation strategies are increasingly common among FEC operators adapting to changing real estate and consumer dynamics. This story offers a practical example of how operators can re-enter a market with a refreshed concept.

articles.title: CONDUCTR and Supercell Launch IP-Driven Interactive Challenge Room Based on Clash of Clans
articles.summary: UK innovation studio CONDUCTR has partnered with mobile gaming giant Supercell to create Clash for the Stache, a physical interactive challenge room experience bringing Clash of Clans into the real world. The experience uses modular gameplay media and software built on CONDUCTR's Arena platform, demonstrating a scalable model for converting popular gaming IP into physical FEC-style attractions. FEC operators exploring IP licensing and interactive room concepts should closely study this activation model.

articles.title: Panasonic and Illumibot Launch AI-Driven Mobile Projection Mapping System for Venues and Events
articles.summary: Panasonic and Illumibot have debuted IllumiBeast, a fleet of AI-driven, projection-enabled mobile units that bring professional-grade projection mapping to venues of any size without large technical teams or fixed infrastructure. The platform uses smartphone cameras and natural language prompts to detect surfaces, generate visuals, and deploy projections, dramatically lowering the barrier to entry for experiential activations. FEC operators and event programmers should evaluate this technology for seasonal activations, special events, and exterior entertainment experiences.

articles.title: Panasonic Partners with Illumibot on AI-Powered Mobile Projection Mapping Units
articles.summary: InPark's coverage of the Panasonic-Illumibot partnership adds technical detail on how the IllumiBeast units use AI and natural language prompts to simplify projection mapping deployment for venues. The technology is positioned as an accessible solution for entertainment venues that want high-impact visual experiences without dedicated AV infrastructure. For FEC operators exploring immersive event upgrades, this platform represents a notably low-friction entry point.

articles.title: InfoComm 2026: AV Industry Shifts Focus Toward Simplicity and Ease of Deployment
articles.summary: At InfoComm 2026 in Las Vegas, a clear theme emerged: the AV industry is increasingly prioritizing ease of deployment, operation, and maintenance alongside raw performance metrics like lumens and resolution. This shift has direct implications for FEC operators and venue designers who need powerful audiovisual systems but often lack dedicated technical staff to manage them. Operators evaluating AV upgrades should look for products that balance capability with operational simplicity.

articles.title: Boxing Arcade Machines Gain Traction as Revenue Drivers in Modern Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: A new report examines how boxing arcade machines are being adopted by entertainment venues as engaging, revenue-generating attractions that appeal to a broad demographic. Redemption and skill-based arcade equipment continues to evolve, and physical interaction machines like boxing units offer differentiation in arcade lineups. FEC operators refreshing their arcade floor should consider the draw of physical competition games in driving repeat visits and incremental revenue.

articles.title: Kentucky Kingdom Acquires Jack O' Lantern Spectacular for Fall Season Programming
articles.summary: Kentucky Kingdom is adding the Jack O' Lantern Spectacular, a major regional fall event in its 14th year, to complement its existing Pumpkins at Kentucky Kingdom festival. The move illustrates how amusement parks and FECs are aggressively building out seasonal event programming to extend their operating calendars and drive fall attendance. Operators looking to boost Q3 and Q4 revenue should study how established seasonal IP can be acquired and layered onto existing events.

articles.title: Legoland California Expands Halloween Event with New Immersive Walkthrough Experience
articles.summary: Legoland California Resort is launching Brick or Treat Street, a new immersive Halloween walkthrough featuring six themed houses as part of its expanded Brick or Treat seasonal event. Seasonal event expansion is a proven strategy for increasing attendance and per-visit spending, and Legoland's approach offers a scalable model applicable to family-focused FECs. Operators planning fall programming can draw inspiration from how the park is layering new experiential content onto an existing event framework.

articles.title: Shooting Outside Dezerland Park Raises Safety and Security Concerns for Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: A 16-year-old has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of a 14-year-old outside Dezerland Park, a large entertainment complex in Orlando. The incident is a stark reminder of the security challenges facing large FEC and entertainment destinations, particularly those that attract youth crowds. Operators should review their external security protocols, crowd management procedures, and relationships with local law enforcement in light of this tragedy.

articles.title: Massanutten Resort Opens New Alpine Coaster Attraction
articles.summary: Virginia's Massanutten Resort has debuted Mountain Mayhem, an alpine coaster featuring 2,156 feet of track and two full 360-degree spirals. Alpine coasters continue to be among the most ROI-efficient thrill attractions for resort and outdoor FEC operators, requiring relatively modest land footprints while delivering strong guest appeal. This opening provides a useful benchmark for operators evaluating outdoor coaster additions.

articles.title: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 Achieves Certified Sustainable Event Status
articles.summary: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 has been certified as a sustainable event following an independent third-party review, achieving a score of 76% against mandatory sustainability requirements. As ESG considerations grow in importance for FEC operators and their corporate partners, industry events setting sustainability benchmarks help normalize responsible practices across the sector. Operators preparing for IAAPA Expo Europe should be aware of the event's sustainability commitments and consider aligning their own practices accordingly.

articles.title: Cirque du Soleil Identifies Three Key Elements That Make Live Experiences Irreplaceable
articles.summary: Cirque du Soleil has published insights from four decades of live entertainment experience, identifying rarity, authenticity, and shared community as the three elements that keep audiences choosing live experiences over digital content. These findings are directly applicable to FEC operators crafting programming and marketing strategies in an increasingly content-saturated world. Understanding what drives guests to choose physical, in-person entertainment is essential for operators looking to sharpen their value proposition.

articles.title: New Research Reveals Preferences and Behaviors of Adults 35-54 Without Children
articles.summary: New consumer research focuses on adults aged 35 to 54 without children, a demographic that is often underserved by FEC marketing but represents significant discretionary spending power. Understanding this audience's entertainment motivations, social behaviors, and preferences for experience types can help FEC operators expand their target demographic beyond the traditional family unit. Operators seeking to grow weeknight and off-peak revenue should pay close attention to this emerging research.
